payload.park_reason: Canonical jump-table infrastructure, not a register/codegen cheat. The 24 asmfix rules (15 renames of GCC .L labels to external jtbl_800108CC + 8 jlabel conversions + 1 delete_between stripping GCC's emitted table) are structurally required: the switch's jump table jtbl_800108CC sits in the FIRST rodata block (asm/data 800.rodata_pre.o, bb2.ld:25, addr 0x800108CC), while code6cac_b2.o's own .rodata links far later (bb2.ld:50). A GCC in-object jump table can only land in code6cac_b2's late rodata, never at 0x108CC, so referencing the external jtbl symbol + deleting GCC's table is mandatory. Zero-asmfix Tier-4 would need relocating the jtbl into code6cac_b2.c AND reordering the whole global rodata layout (project-wide, needs user sign-off). This is the project standard for the 26 jtbl-rename functions whose tables splat carved into asm/data rodata. Separately, sandbox cannot score this file: file-wide tier-3 stripping shifts maspsx indices and crashes a sibling fn's index-based regfix reorder rule (KeyError in tools/regfix.py).

payload.park_reason: Pure-C distance 1. Single gap: GCC fill_eager_delay_slots DUPLICATES the post-loop 'andi v1,s1,0xFF' (count==2 mask) into the beqz (count!=0 guard) delay slot (+1 insn); target uses simple fall-through fill with i=0. A pure-C 'for (i=0; (i&0xFF)<count; i++)' loop reproduces the EXACT target structure (verified from cc1 -da output: i=0 in beqz delay slot, single post-loop andi, matching regalloc s0/s1/s2/s3). BUT that form changes func_8005BD30's GCC auto-label count, which renumbers .L labels file-wide and breaks SIBLING asmfix delete_between rules (func_8006BD28 .L8006BDD4, func_80070C70 .L80070D7C no longer match) -> retire breaks the oracle. Needs file-wide label stabilization (architecture/escalate), not closable in a single-function pass. (do-while-inside-if is the only sibling-safe form and forces the eager-fill the 3 regfix rules undo.)

Pure-C distance 1; a verified pure-C for-loop form retires all 3 regfix rules. SOTN fix = RETIRE those sibling bridges to pure C (eliminating their label-anchored cheats); then this lands with nothing to break. Do NOT stabilize the sibling cheat anchors -- that hardens cheats, against Tier-4.

payload.park_reason: Pure-C form READY and byte-identical standalone (sandbox --disable all = 0; full-register objdump vs canonical = 0/81 diffs), but blocked by global .L-label drift into 3 siblings. Fix: thread a separate flag var (s32 result) instead of re-testing count==-1 at the done block: on the 3rd-call success set result=0 and goto done; on loop exhaust set result=-1 (reuses the loop's -1 sentinel left in v0); done dispatch is if(result!=0)return 0. Must use a SEPARATE var from the success branch condition or GCC threads the success edge straight to the call (collapsing the bnez merge -> distance 6). The matching C consumes ONE FEWER global .L label than HEAD (cc1: max .L 232 vs 233, 94 defs both), shifting every later system.c sibling -1. Breaks hardcoded-absolute-.L cheats: marionation_Exec (subst .L128->.L999 @111/@129 + asmfix beq .L128), saEft00Add (subst .L199+4 @25, .L207+4 @92 + asmfix bne/.L199, beq/.L207); cpu_side_move_dir_4 labels also shift. Observed: full-build SHA1 a688747729dc... (canonical 62efab...), sole diffs = marionation_Exec idx133/154 and saEft00Add idx26/102 branch targets off by 0x10. Resolution is a POLICY decision touching 3 sibling functions (bump their hardcoded .L by -1 and SHA1-verify, OR de-cheat the siblings to pure C). Per .claude/rules/global-label-drift-sibling-cheat.md.
